title: Superb safari
text: What an excellent trip! Our best ever safari- a total of 29 wild dogs seen on 5 separate days,6 leopard, 5 cheetah ,numerous lions not to mention fabulous elephants . Plenty of other animals including a pair of marsh mongooses( a rare sighting) and many birds. A wonderful guide in Shaka  and superb camp staff- how Vicks produced the meals he did was amazing and we were so well looked after by Tony and Kennedy.If we ever return it will definitely be with Letaka

title: Get close to nature
text: 9 night mobile camping safari with 3 camps in Moremi, Marabe and Savuti as a small group tour of six. Incredible experience with Mbara an excellent driver/guide who could find anything and was very knowledgeable about behaviour as well as identification (including birds which is unusual).  Kennedy, Vicks and Tony set up and managed the camps and provided excellent food, including fresh baked bread every day, with minimal facilities. Would thoroughly recommend to anyone wanting to get close to nature and happy to forgo a jacuzzi!

title: Outstanding in every way!
text:
Our 6 night “Miracle Rivers” mobile safari with Letaka was absolutely outstanding!

•	The team we travelled with was amazing – genuine, personable and such a pleasure to have around camp. Our guide Mbara was exceptional and the key to our successful sightings. He has a deep knowledge of the local wildlife and their behaviour and used his skills to fulfill our wish lists and more – we saw everything there was to see. He was fun, enthusiastic, a good communicator, a fountain of knowledge, and passionate about Botswana and its wildlife. 
•	Mealtimes were a highlight – all the food was delicious and healthy. Dinner was preceded by an evening campfire with drinks and reliving the day’s adventures, until our cook arrived to announce the evening’s menu and we moved over to the beautifully set dinner table.
•	Accommodations are very comfortable… this is glamping at its finest! A bathroom with toilet and bucket shower (which works really well) attached to the back of the tent, comfortable beds, warm water delivered to our tent at 5 am every morning for washing up… we wanted for nothing.
•	The vehicles that Letaka uses are the best: Toyota Land Cruisers that are seemingly indestructible. With a maximum of 7 guests on each trip (and only five on ours), these vehicles, with seating for 8 in the back and another one beside the guide, provide plenty of space.

I could go on, but suffice to say we would highly recommend Letaka to anyone considering a mobile safari in Botswana. I’d been dreaming of doing a safari since I was a teenager and this trip did not disappoint: we loved the whole experience and were blown away by the abundance of wildlife. We hope to go back!

title: Unforgettable experience on a mobile safari
text:
My wife and I went on Letaka Safari's ten-day, Kasane-Savuti-Mababe-Moremi mobile safari and it was life-affirming!  From pick-up to drop-off the tour was run professionally and smoothly.  The wildlife viewing was better than anything I could have expected; huge amount of game in natural settings, behaving naturally, and seemingly completely undisturbed by our vehicle.  The marvellous viewing was made possible by our incredible guide Benjamin who was well connected and respected by other guides, amazing at spotting game, and sensitive about approaching the animals and positioning our vehicle perfectly.  As a birder I was also delighted that he really knew his birds!  All this, and he was just a delightful human being!  The campsites were isolated and beautiful, and run by a team of supporters that made camp life run well and delightful!  Our cook produced extraordinary meals on open fires and coals; the days started and ended with delightful campfires.  All the little details were attended to and the staff tried to accomodate every request.  The food was an unexpected highlight.  The tents and beds were comfortable and spacious

Is a mobile safari for everybody?  No, but I don't want experiences that appeal to everyone.  A few things you need to know about:  Botswana in September is very hot, so hot that you really can't do anything for 4 or 5 hours mid-day, and the wildlife is dormant.  The positive of this is that the mornings and evenings are a lovely temperature.  Relatedly, game drives start very early in the morning, at dawn, so if getting up at 5:30 am isn't what you want to do on vacation, reconsider.  These really are game drives, so one spends a LOT of time bouncing around dusty sandy roads in an open Toyota Land Cruiser.  It may be up to 6 to 8 hours a day in the vehicle, so you have to be OK with that amount of time pretty much confined to the vehicle.  And yes it is hot and dusty.
For me the opportunity to be immersed in the Botswanan environment and the incrediboe access to a staggering amount of game more than compensated for these issues, but its best to be aware of them.
An added bonus was getting to meet the three other guests on the safari.  We all got along very well and will keep in touch.  
Memories for a lifetime!

title: Letaka safaris, an amazing experience
text:
We have just returned home from our trip to Botswana and Zimbabwe which included a Letaka 6 night mobile safari in the Okavango Delta n September.
I wanted to share our experience of the Letaka mobile safari and to thanks them for providing us with such an incredible and memorable experience. Our guide Mbara was exceptional; personable, fun and he appeared to possess an almost telepathic understanding of where to see and spot wildlife. His understanding of animal behaviour was amazing. Time and time again he manoeuvred us into positions where the animals subsequently appeared. I don't think it was chance that enabled us to spend time with lion, cheetah, leopard and my particular wish list wild dogs. It was down to his skill, knowledge and expertise. We were also blessed with so many other sightings of animals and birds. Everyday there was something new and exciting to see.
Our safari was memorable not just because of Mbara, the team looking after us were brilliant too. Chef Life, KK and Squad were wonderful. We were treated like royalty. The food, the locations and the camps all combined to make an amazing experience. I find it hard to imagine that there is a better team out there!  I would have no hesitation whatsoever in thoroughly recommending Letaka safaris to anyone else.

title: An incredible experience
text: We had high expectations for Botswana and our 9 night Northern Highlights mobile camping trip - and this trip far exceeded that!  From the amazing Lataka team to the incredible game viewing, to the beautiful camp sites. The humor, knowledge and experience of Shaka and his team - Life the chef, and KK and Squad was exceptional. They worked tirelessly to ensure that we were extremely well fed with delicious food (inc. cakes, bread and biscuits), very comfortable in camp and always a G&T sundowner at hand at the end of an exciting game viewing day.  We lost count of the elephant, wildebeest, zebra and were so thrilled at the amazing lions sightings, leopard, cheetah and painted dogs. A truly magical experience!

title: A wonderful experience with Letaka Safaris
text: We enjoyed a 12 day "extended Mobile Safari" in Botswana in September, our first experience of this company and really enjoyed the experience.  The trip started in Maun with a night at the Thamalakane River Lodge which was first class.  Here we enjoyed an evening river cruise spotting a variety of wildlife before the sun came down (note, this cruise was nothing to do with Letaka but something we spotted on offer from the Lodge).  The following day we flew to Xakamaxa with MackAir where we met our driver/guide, Benjamin who would look after us for the next ten days.  The safari took us to three camp sites - Xakanaxa, Mababe and Savuti where we would stay - three nights per camp.  Each day included morning and evening game drives where we saw an excellent variety of wildlife.  The camp-sites were excellent and we were well looked after by the additional team of three.  The food was basic but very good and was accompanied in the evening with pre-dinner sundowners and wine with the meal.  There was also a one hour Macoro ride (2 to a boat) at Khwai during our transit from Xakanaxa to Mababe. The safari concluded in Kasane where we enjoyed a river boat cruise on the Chobe River with, again, a wide variety of wildlife. It was a very enjoyable trip which i would recommend to anyone.

title: Letaka Mobile Safari
text: Fabulous experience - a very well organised nine day mobile safari. Our guide, Vincent's  enthusiasm for the wildlife was infectious.  He was so knowledgeable and always knew the best place to position the jeep to get the best view of them, from the smallest bird to the largest elephant!  The team  at the camp, KB, PK and William looked after us so well too.  All four of the camp team had a great sense of humour. Thank you Vincent and team for making this such a memorable experience.

title: Botswana highlights
text:
Fantastic wildlife sightings including very close and prolonged views of Leopard and Cheetah.
Alex , our guide first class on bird identification and excellent company!

title: Botswana Letaka mobile camping safari
text:
We had the most incredible time and the holiday was so well organised. The team that looked after us was brilliant. Food was amazing and there was a good supply of cold drinks both in the vehicle and in camp, which were needed as temperatures went above 40C. 
Our guide Vincent was very knowledgable and had a wonderful sense of humour. He was able to spot, track and identify wildlife whilst negotiating bumpy tracks. Not only did he find the animals but he gave us time to observe their behaviour, which was truly amazing. We saw leopards, lions, cheetahs, hippos, elephants, zebras, wild dogs, hyenas, giraffes, various antelopes, reptiles and many other species including over 100 different birds eg secretary bird, Kori bustard, raptors, owls - the list goes on. One of the most memorable highlights was the murmuration of millions of red billed quelea.
The rest of the camp team ( KB, PK and William) were brilliant, very well organised and cheerful. Chef was fantastic and produced amazing meals on the campfire, including bread and cakes for afternoon tea.
This has been a holiday to remember forever. Thank you Vincent and his Letaka team for looking after us so well - you are up there with the best.

title: Live Your Dreams - Letaka manages this spectacularly!
text:
Live your dreams Spread your wings - words that Letaka ensure become reality and then some! Our memories of this world class trip will last forever. I can’t say enough about Letaka from the minute we made the initial enquiry to our trip they were second to none.  Heili in the office answered all our questions and made sure we had everything we asked for on the trip (this included a special coffee drink). Jonathan located our luggage that British Airways managed to lose and arranged for it to be flown directly into the Okavango Delta.  This was an incredible feat of logistical planning and we were so grateful to receive our luggage after just one day.  From the moment the four of us began our adventure, we were extremely well looked after by the Fab Four - our guide KG was a force of everything that is good in a human  - so knowledgeable, kind, patient - answered all our questions and we asked a lot 🤪and always ensured we had the most amazing views of the wildlife.   I don’t know how he does it but we always had the best view and to say we were in amongst the wildlife is hard to explain - at one point we had approx 60 hyenas around the vehicle - we were truly immersed yet KG was always respectful of the animals’ space and never upset the natural dynamics of the wildlife’s behaviour.  Vicks, our Chef, made us the most sensational 3 course meals every night.  From stews to filo parcels to fresh salads to lemon meringue - Yummy! Tony and Kennedy looked after all our needs around camp.  We had hot water for our bucket showers, beds were made and washing was done.  Water to wash in and water to drink.  Fires prepared and stoked.  Fresh coffee kept warm with the fire embers.  We couldn’t have coped with the cold mornings without those hot water bottles.   Leboga Letaka ! 
We saw a pride of lions - 17 in all - at one time, alongside another few later in the week.  As mentioned, approx 60 hyenas - what an event!   Cheetahs and cubs - with Mama cheetah showing the babies how to hunt.   A magnificent leopard who leapt in the water behind us and elephants - so many - Bulls, Matriach and funny youngsters getting in cheeky trouble.  Buffalo, wilderbeast, giraffe, zebra and crocodiles and hippos.  Wild dogs!  We saw them all!   We had visitors in camp and at no point were we scared - the super calm of KG and his expertise meant we knew we were in the best hands!   KG - we salute you Sir and hope to see you again.   Thank you team Letaka for the dream holiday.   You are simply the best!   The Gaunt family x

title: Fantastic safari experience!
text:
I did the northern highlights southbound tour with Letaka in May and it was an amazing trip.  I have done multiple safaris and this ranked up at the top.  Botswana felt more "wild" and less crowded than the other parks I have been to.  Our guides (Bate and Alex) and camp crew were incredible.  This was my first camping safari but it was so comfortable and the food was top notch.  We ended up with two guides as one had a family emergency and both were so good at tracking game and positioning the vehicle in the perfect spot for viewing and photography.  

I was hoping to see wild dogs as I hadn't seen them on my previous safaris and they certainly delivered.  We saw a group hunting in Savuti and then spent two days with them in Mababe.  If you are eager to see them I was told the best chances is going between late May to July as that is when they typically den.  We spent time at their den, saw two hunts/kills, saw them fight off both hyenas and a crocodile, saw how they communicated with either other, and more.  We of course had many other amazing sightings including lions, cheetah, and leopard.

title: Magical birthday trip in Botswana
text:
10 day  camping safari was trip of a lifetime .Our guide KG was the best - funny and knowledgeable . Camp crew excellent. No single supplement on our trip
Saw so much game - leopards . Cheetah . Wild dogs Hippos  even  lion cubs on my birthday

title: An experience of a lifetime!
text: What an truly incredible experience had traveling with Letaka Safaris.  After having “glamped” so many times throughout Africa I decided to try a mobile camping safari.  All reviews led to the same company, Letaka was always the most highly recommended and I was not disappointed.  Everything from my comfy bed to delicious meals, thrilling game drives and starry nights were perfect.  But, most of all was the incredible staff that always had a smile on their faces and made all our comforts in the “bush” possible.  Evidence always had a cheerful wake up every morning. He made sure our showers were hot after lunch and our beds were made and the towels were always carefully arranged.  The “bush baby” (Hot water bottle) every night was perfect.  K was the smiling face for every meal and made sure we had eaten enough and enjoyed an extra glass of wine.  His goodbyes as we headed off on a game drive and our welcome back iced tea were always so appreciated.  He was such a joy.  Ludick, our chef, was a miracle worker.  In a  “bush kitchen “ he managed to create wonderful interesting and varied lunches, dinners and picnics.  We definitely ate well with fresh baked bread every day and a fresh cake every day for our tea… every dinner started with a delicious starter and a varied buffet.  The salads were always fresh and crispy and a different dessert every evening. I loved his rusks, peanut butter and chocolate chip cookies we enjoyed every day when we stopped for coffee, tea and hot chocolate mid morning game drive. Bait, our trusted guide, was a wealth of information.  His knowledge of everything from animals, birds, vegetation, the stars, etc. was incredible.  He made sure we missed nothing and was so patient with all of our needs.  He made sure that we searched out the very best sightings and was so dedicated to making sure that we all had an excellent view.  He gave each of us the time we needed to not only observe but to wait for that special picture.  If we happened on another safari Bait was also considerate and  made sure that they also had the opportunity to get a good view.  He always scoped out the best places for  our mid morning coffee and cookie break and our Sundowners were always in spectacular places.  His great personality, knowledge and love of Botswana made our safari all that i had hoped for.  Can’t thank Letaka enough for putting together such a great itinerary that gave us the opportunity to enjoy 3 such diverse camps and the team that made our safari an experience of a lifetime.

title: Fabulous safari experince
text: We had an amazing safari holiday with Letaka Safaris. Our expert guide, Tumi, gave us a fabulous game viewing experience, with leopard ( and cubs), cheetah (and cubs), wild dogs with pups and a kill, lions (and cubs), and of course, lots of elephants, in and out of the water! The camp staff were so thoughtful, kind and attentive, food was great - we had a wonderful time.

The trip with Letaka Safaris was above all expectations. First of all this adventure was not a "glamping" experience. The accommodations are what you might consider military style tents, for lack of a better word. The tents at these private campsites are dismantled  every 2 or three days and reassembled at the next location where you find them set up at the end of the day. These tents were clean and well appointed for their intended purpose. If you are looking for an experience to get the feeling of what an Africa safari was like in days gone by this is the perfect choice.
     The professional service was outstanding from the greeting at the airport to the end of the trip. The safari is limited to a total of 7 persons and you were the only ones at each camp site. There are two game drives every day. Our guide was fantastic. The food was fantastic, all cooked with wood, including fresh bread every day. The entire staff was knowledgeable, personable and overall enjoyable to be with. I cannot say enough about  all the excellent experiences all of our group had on this 9 day adventure. This safari  can qualify as a " trip of a lifetime". Thank you Letaka.

title: Outstanding safari trip
text: Incredibly competent team looked after us whilst in Botswana. Shaka was a first rate driver and guide, who was in his element seeking out both major predators and other wildlife during our trips around Chobe and other parks. Inspite of the terrain and conditions we never felt other than totally confident in him. The rest of the team provided brilliant camp cooking, had comfortable tents and bedding ready for our arrival and ensured that Inspite of the remoteness of our journey, there were plenty of home comforts - including hot water bottles for the chill African nights!

title: Safari Trip to remember for a lifetime
text: Amazing 8-nights (We trimmed down 1 from the Northern highlights itinerary) hard-core safari expedition, the mobile tented safari with Letaka was truly  a once in a lifetime experience. Game Drives across Savuti, Mababe & Moremi, Boat cruise on the Chobe river, Mokoro Ride in Khwai - Every day was an adventure. While the nights and mornings were really cold, the fantastic game drives and camping with the crew made it all worthwhile. KG is an extremely competent (and ethical) guide and driver. We saw all the animals we set out to see. (Beyond general game, close encounters with wild dogs, Cheetahs, a Leopard and almost daily sightings of lions) Being local, a part of a network of guides, and overall familiarity with the area and animal behaviour really makes a huge difference in locating them. He was careful in explaining how he was going to position our jeep and what we needed to do; we could get close and it still felt that the animals were almost never bothered with our presence. Also, he comes across as a super confident driver and overall car expert, ensuring we had no Jeep issues in the entirety of the trip. We can see how easy cars may get stuck in the sand, mud, water crossings etc, and on some occasions KG has helped others quickly with either dispensing advice or a quick pull from our jeep. Camp crew were super helpful and nice, it was great seeing Elliot and Jean everyday, and especially KK, which we looked most forward to, as it meant we were going to be treated to amazing meals cooked with his bush oven. Overall, this is one for the bucket-list and will provide the family with memories for a lifetime. Thank you Letaka

ownerResponse.text: Amazing to see how well you enjoyed the safari, thank you!

title: Letaka Safaris is the best!!
text:
We just returned from a fantastic 6-day mobile camping trip in May with Letaka Safaris in Botswana!  We saw an unbelievable amount of wildlife, including lions, leopards, cheetahs, elephants, giraffes, zebras, warthogs, buffalo, several species of antelope, painted dogs, crocodiles, monitor lizards……. and we identified close to 100 different species of birds.  Our guide, Benjamin, said we were very lucky, but we strongly believe it was much more likely because of his remarkable knowledge of the bush and his ability to predict when and where to see the animals!  He was also very mindful of getting us to the very best views and lighting so that we could get good photographs, many even with cell phones!  He never put us in any danger, but got us right in the middle of some incredible wildlife encounters. Benjamin was the best!! 

Camp life was great;  we enjoyed delicious meals cooked outside by an always helpful group of guys, as well as morning coffee/tea breaks and gin and tonic sundowners while on the game drives.  We had fresh-baked breads and cakes, as well as delicious three-course meals.  The beds were comfortable and cozy for the cool nights, and listening to the sounds of the bush at night was magical.  A camping safari might not be for everyone, but for us it was perfect.

We completed our trip to Africa with a two-night stay at a lodge and two nights in Victoria Falls.  Although those were also great, the authenticity of the mobile camping safari couldn't be beat.  Six days with a small group of like-minded individuals made for a truly exceptional adventure that we will never forget.

ownerResponse.text:
Hi Lilian, your words inspire us to keep doing what we do! 
Thank you!!

title: This is the safari to choose if you want an immersive experience!
text: We chose the Letaka 9 day mobile safari in Botswana and could not have been happier!  The staff was just the best, especially our guide, Benjamin Ndjavera.  He knows the answer to every question and knows how to track animals and call birds.  The other 3 staff (Willy, KB and Kaki) were incredible, setting up our tents, waiting on us and cooking the most amazing foods from scratch with no water or electricity!  We truly were blown away by the exceptional service from this crew.  The beds were comfortable and the camp showers were hot.  The safari vehicle was wonderful with graduated seating so that all could see.  There were plenty of plugs to charge all our devices and batteries.  Top notch all around!

ownerResponse.text: Thank you so much for the lovely review, Please return to Botswana again soon!

title: Beyond Expectations
text:
We have just completed the North to South 10 day tented safari with Letaka. Amazing doesn’t describe the trip we had. The whole experience ran so smoothly  from pick up in Kasane to drop off in Maun. Great Crew under the leadership of Vincent and his team of Leif, KK and Squad, Vincent went beyond expectations to find animals, birds and water wildlife. The kitchen produced all manner of delights even for my vegetarian friend.  Wow can’t recommend them enough. 
We went and met Hayley in the back office , plus Brett one of the owners who were so welcoming, thanks guys

ownerResponse.text: Lovely jubbly, thanks for the support and return soon to Botswana, please :)

title: Fabulous safari in Botswana
text:
We did the 9 day extended safari  with Letaka, visiting Moremi, Mubabe and Savuti. It was a wonderful experience.  Our guide Vinny was exceptional; he got us close to the animals while always respecting their space and was a font of enormous knowledge. He also had a great sense of humour so the drives were a lot of fun as well as extremely educational. The camp was looked after by a team of 3. The chef, Life, was incredible - we had a wide variety of food cooked in his bush kitchen and he was able to produce fabulously tasty food for us every day.  Keke and Squawd completed the team and they were so lovely to come home to at the end of a drive.  Always cheerful and ready with the iced tea and an extra bucket of warm water for the bucket shower or ready to fix anything that needed sorting like the tent zip… nothing was too much trouble. 
Most days were very well balanced between activity and rest, every afternoon included a chance for a siesta.  The last day was a bit arduous as it is a very long drive in an open safari truck to Kasane. 
Overall this is a really well organised company with delightful staff and if you want to see animals in their natural environment this is ‘real’ safari not gated reserves, and staying in tents without fencing means you are properly immersed.

ownerResponse.text: So happy to see how well you enjoyed the safari, thanks for the review!

title: Once in a lifetime safari in Botswana with Letaka safaris
text:
We had a spectacular extended mobile safari 9 night fairly luxurious camping),which was booked through Llama travel and hosted by Letaka Safaris in Botswana.
We flew from Joburg to Maun then via a light aircraft to Moremi where we met our guide.
The safari was led by Vinny who was an absolute expert on the wildlife and showed great skill in always positioning our vehicle so we had the best views of the animals and birds we saw so that we could get great photos. He was ably supported by a great team in camp including Leif, (a fantastic chef), KK and Sqard who worked tirelessly to provide us with everything we needed. All of the Letaka people were attentive to our needs, interesting to chat with and had great senses of humour which all added to our enjoyment. We stayed in Moremi, Mababe and Savuti reserves and saw leopards, lions, elephant, hippos etc. etc.
The travel between reserves was at times bumpy on the unveven sandy roads and there was one long day driving from Savuti to Kasane which was a bit of an endurance test (Suggest maybe there could be flight for this leg ?), but all in all it was a well planned and varied mix of driving safaris, boat trips and a couple of nights in comfortable lodges. Our trip was topped off with two days in Zimbabwe visiting the Victoria Falls which was simply amazing!
If you can get Vinny as your guide with the rest of the team you are guaranteed a fantastic holiday.
We loved it all!

ownerResponse.text:
Many thanks for the feedback, glad the safari was enjoyed thoroughly. 
The charter flight out from Savuti to Kasane is something that is doable but due to the distance, it turns out to be very expensive leg of the journey... border line same rate as a long haul international flight. Shall keep it in mind though. 
thanks again and please do return to Botswana soon!

title: Fantastic tented safari in Botswana with Letaka Safaris, booked through Llama Travel in the UK
text:
An excellent 9 night mobile tented safari in Moremi, Mababe and Savuti in Botswana with Letaka.  The 4 man crew was led by Vincent.  They were all wonderful.  Vinny had great empathy with the wildlife and he made sure we saw everything we could and kept us entertained and well informed.  Great company.  The food was excellent, all cooked by Live, the chef and we will never forget the wonderful cakes he made.  The other 2 crew supported the guide and chef and made sure we were all welcome and well catered for.
An incredible experience not to be missed.  Highly recommended.

ownerResponse.text: Highly appreciative of your lovely review, thank you Bob.

title: Perfect Photographic Safari
text:
Letaka is an award-winning Safari operation based in Botswana.  I have done four of their mobile tented Safaris now and each one has been brilliant in all respects;  their reputation is fully justified.   

My most recent trip in April 2025 was a 9 - nights “Shutter Safari” geared for photographers, moving camp across three sites in the Okavango Delta/ Chobe River regions. 

A photographic safari:

-	For anyone interested in photographing wildlife, it is great to know that your group will be made up of like-minded people who are happy to linger at interesting scenes, to pay attention to things like the direction and quality of the light, and to observe interesting interactions between animals and other behaviours;  and that the Guide understands these priorities too. 
-	Letaka deserves credit for going ahead with a trip that had only two of us on board;  usually they are geared up for 6 or 7 visitors.  With just two of us, we had unimpeded views and could easily agree what our priorities were for getting the best shots. 

The itinerary and structure:

-	I really like the format of the mobile tented safari, moving across three locations, spending 3 nights in each.  It works well, providing some diversity of habitat and wildlife with sufficient time in each place to get a feel for what makes it special. 
-	The daily routine works well too, with an early start,  long morning drive and a shorter evening drive and rest in between.   This allows us to experience the most favourable light as well as the periods of the day when the animals are most active, other of course than at night. 
-	It was an added bonus to be able to do two night drives in Mabebe, which is not subject to all the restrictions of the national parks.

Letaka Team:

-	The team of Guide [Bate], Cook [Poster], and Assistants [Dasong and Scarra] worked really well together, and as far as I could tell had a great team spirit. They were unfailingly friendly, cheerful and attentive to our comfort all the way through, from the first warm welcome, to the final goodbyes.  
-	It was a privilege to be invited to their quarters to see how they worked the miracle of the cooking as well as the other systems such as water purification, refrigeration, vehicle maintenance etc that all were essential to a successful trip. 
-	Bate is not just an exceptional guide, but also a great companion.  He completely understands what we wanted on a photographic safari, in terms of spending time with the animals, appreciating the light, positioning the vehicle so get the best views. I was impressed not just with his remarkable spotting and tracking skills, but also his anticipation of how animals might move into and out of a scene, how they might interact with each other and display certain types of behaviour.    He not only reads the tracks and traces of animal movement, but is able to communicate very clearly to visitors why and how he is doing this, so that we call start to join in.  The “looking for” the animals becomes as much of the trip as the “looking at”.   We felt engaged all the way. 
-	Bate was able to answer any question that we threw at him.  He is regularly checking in to see that we are OK, that we are happy with how the day is going, presenting us with options and alternatives if the original plan does not seem to be delivering what we expected.   

The equipment and facilities:
-	The vehicle, a customised Landcruiser,  does very well in the terrain.  The cool box is very efficient, as are the battery-charging points – essential when running off so many shots. 
-	The tents are very spacious and well designed, with built in “porch”, ground sheets, windows  and  “en suite” shower and toilet, which is very reassuring on a dark night with animals potentially nearby.  The LED lights work well.  The “Directors Chairs” in front of the tents and round the camp fire are very comfortable.  
-	People thinking about a mobile tented safari camping as an alternative to using lodges should be reassured that this is a very comfortable version of camping! 
-	The dining area set up works very well – a useful shelter, solid seats and a large table, with tea, coffee and other refreshments always available.   

The food [last but definitely not least]:
-	I have done four Letaka camping Safaris and the food is always superb.   How the cooks – in this case, Poster – manage to produce such a range of dishes every day with no repetition is remarkable.  There is also an impressive range of alcoholic and non-alcoholic drinks on offer.  

I definitely plan to visit again with Letaka!

ownerResponse.text:
Hi Robert, thank you again for another attendance on a Letaka Safari!
You are a certified Repeat Offender for adventure with Letaka. ;)
We look forward to your next safari! 
Thanks again Robert!

title: Greatest adventure!!
text:
We took a safari with Letaka in April, 2025. It was the most amazing adventure!!! I was unsure of this trip for months before we left. My fears were soon dissolved when we boarded the Land Cruiser and began our journey to the first campsite. On the relatively short drive, we encountered many impala, baboons, hippos, giraffes, elephants and lions.
Each day started early (5 am) with breakfast and a game drive. Again, we were treated with an array of animals. It was truly amazing how close we were able to get to the animals, while still respecting their space. Our guide, Vincent, was very knowledgeable and knew just where to take us for viewing the wildlife. 
Camp life soon became comfortable thanks to our most wonderful crew. Ludick was an excellent chef. I was impressed at the quality of food that came out of the 'camp kitchen'. I think he worked magic, every lunch and dinner was delicious! Karabo, the bartender was always ready to take an order for almost anything you wanted to drink, and Evidence, our camp tender would make sure your shower and wash basin was ready with warm water for your use when you returned from a game drive. He also kept the tents clean and did any laundry needed. 
I would absolutely recommend Letaka for an adventure you will never forget.

title: Fantastic camping safari
text: I recently returned from a 10 days/9 nights camping safari in Botswana’s Okavango Delta and it was a fabulous experience. Our safari guide Benjamin was excellent and with him doing some brilliant tracking and driving, plus a bit of luck on our side we were on the spot to observe not one but three kills in as many days: (1) four lions who took down a zebra; (2) a pack of wild African dogs who demolished an Impala in about 3 minutes; and (3) a leopard which stalked and killed an Impala. We saw so much wonderful wildlife - masses of elephants, lions, zebras, giraffes, impalas, buffalo, hippos, water buck. It had rained a lot recently (but not while we were there) and the landscape was lush & green and stunning. We were looked after extremely well by 3 camp staff. The chef produced delicious 3 course dinners, lunches, fresh salads, cakes, all from a grill over an open fire and sand ovens. Every 3 days the entire camp was moved and set up again in the new location with great efficiency. At one site, I wasn’t happy with the position of my tent so while we were out on a game drive, the staff set up a new one for me. Everything ran very smoothly and I would happily recommend Letaka. In fact, one of my travelling companions had done 4 safaris with them previously so they must be doing something right. I might have to return myself.

title: Memories for a lifetime!
text:
I was able to experience Botswana on a 10-day, 9-night safari with Letaka Safaris in February, and it was incredible!  I had stayed in a few lodges before this, but experiencing it from a tent was a whole different adventure.  You feel a lot more immersed in the experience when at night you can hear the elephants grazing close by or lions communicating with each other.  Also, being there in the rainy season made travel a different and more exhilarating experience; you just had to be prepared for it.

The team does a great job of taking care of everyone and everything.  I marvel at their efficiency in setting up and taking down camp every 3 days, regardless of weather conditions, all with a smile on their face.  The chef created delicious vegetarian meals and it's amazing what they are able to prepare with limited facilities.  Mealtimes were always great for conversations about what we had seen and heard, but also getting to know and understand more about everyone around the table and the areas they called home.

It really felt like we had a lot of time at our wildlife sightings, and we had some spectacular and rare ones.  Mbara was a fantastic guide and tracker.  He taught us how to "read the newspaper" i.e. the tracks on the roads, then was able to follow them, find the animal, get us in the perfect spot, and tell us the story of what happened.  He was interested and enthusiastic about the sightings and also keen on furthering his knowledge, with many guidebooks available for all of us to use.

The whole team was incredible, ready to handle whatever happened, professional but also very personable.  I would absolutely recommend travelling with Letaka Safaris, it was a dream come true for me from start to finish.

title: I will never book with this company again!
text: I had never been on safari before, but since I have friends who are avid adventure travellers, my girlfriend and I took a once in a lifetime safari trip to the Okavango Delta in November 2024. We booked with Letaka Safaris who came highly recommend. Although the planning was good and there was plenty to see on each game drive, the customer service was horrible. The camp staff were always distracted and disengaged, leaving us frustrated and undervalued for the entire trip. I doubt that the owners are fostering a motivating, supportive, and inclusive work environment for their employees, which is very sad.

ownerResponse.text:
Hi There, 
Please get in touch with us (info@letakasafaris.com) to discuss your safari, dates, guide name etc as we would like to look into your comments a bit more. 
Many thanks
Letaka Reservations

title: Nine days of sheer pleasure.
text: Letaka safaris are a credit to the safari tour world. Every detail taken care of and with brilliant staff.  We had 9 days camping in Botswana with them and it couldn't have been more perfect. An amazing amount of animals close up in various locations. Our guide, Benjamin, was the best guide that we have had the pleasure to be with. His knowledge and ability to get us into the right positions for photography was excellent. We also had a photographic expert with us, Charlie Summers, who, I have to say was really good. We learnt a lot from him, but more than that, he was an affable and patient character. Happy to offer advice at every stage.

title: Book-ended by Leopards
text:
We chose Letaka based on a recommendation and this trip certainly was all we wanted – actually exceeding our expectations. We did the 9 night safari from Kasane to Maun. There were some long days travelling between areas but this all added to the authentic safari experience. Driving through rather than flying in to selected hot spots gives a much better overall understanding of this wonderful region. 

We began with a leopard and ended with a leopard.  On the first day, as we were approaching Camp 1 in Savuti, we came across a leopard and two cubs – and, then, lurking in the background was a Brown Hyena (yes, a Brown Hyena!), hackles rising.  Needless to say, with all this drama going on, we were late arriving at camp, but were given a warm welcome by the camp team.  In total, we saw six leopards whilst on safari, finishing with a good view of a leopard in a tree on our way to the airstrip on the last day.

Alex was our guide, and what a magnificent guide he was, too.  Through his tracking skills and dogged determination, we had some top-notch wildlife sightings.  It was Alex, after 3½ hours’ driving following Wild Dog tracks, who discovered the pack asleep under a tree.  We returned at 5 pm when they were still sleeping – but at 5.20 they began to rouse.  It was playtime and exercise time before they set off for the evening, stopping to drink.  It was a magical moment. 

Alex’s Wild Dog tracking skills were repeated in Moremi.  Guides in other vehicles had spotted tracks in the morning but had not been able to find the pack.  In the afternoon, we went to the area and, with Alex concentrating on the tracks, and his two willing recruits scanning the bushes, we found them!  We were in time to watch them frolicking around after their daytime sleep, then set off on a hunt – a thrilling experience!

We saw five cheetah and lions galore; we particularly enjoyed hearing all about the Northern Pride and the Marsh Pride in Savuti.  But it wasn’t just the big cats that were a treat.  We loved seeing all the other mammals and birds.  Alex has an excellent knowledge of photography, so knew exactly where to position the vehicle to get the best photo angles.

Our safari experience was just what we wanted.  Gone are the days when we would be happy to put up our two-man tent and cook over a small camping stove.  The Letaka tents were exactly the right level of luxury for us.  We were thoroughly spoiled by our camp team of Ludick, Scara and Poster. This team looked after us wonderfully and we always had a friendly welcoming homecoming every time we returned to camp.  The food was first-class and it was fascinating to have a tour of the bush kitchen on the last day, to see where all the meals were produced.  All in all, five-star luxury that would be difficult to match.

title: AN AMAZING, LIFE ENHANCING EXPERIENCE
text: If it’s a proper, authentic safari experience in Botswana you’re after, look no further. Everything about our 10-day trip was beautifully organised with superb hospitality and a genuine passion for ensuring we got the very best out of the trip. Our guide Vincent was an absolute legend (faultless) and the team of three supporting staff - including a superb chef (still not sure how he managed to produce such high quality meal after meal) - were a total delight. Our tents were basic but perfectly comfortable and, although I spotted better looking vehicles, ours did the job and did it well. Some may prefer a little more by way of home comforts but I’m very happy to have had the amazing, mobile safari experience offered by Letaka rather than staying in just another lodge or lodges. Note: some of the days on our itinerary were very long, and I doubt this tour would suit those with mobility issues or who like the aforementioned home comforts.

title: FABULOUS TENTED SAFARI - 5+ STARS
text: FABULOUS!!  Cannot rate Letaka Tented Safari enough, from the animals (of course) to the food it was all way more than 5 star.  Mbara, Squard, Life and Paki (our guide and hosts) were wonderful - thank you guys.  Very attentive and solicitous and very friendly.  We 2 couples were rewarded with everything you could want from  a safari.  If we had a complaint - and it is only a VERY SMALL ONE - the food is too good and too plentiful!!!

title: Pure nature experience that makes an impression!
text:
I spent nine nights with Letaka on a mobile tent safari. 
I can recommend the trip to anyone, because the experience was formative and absolutely great. Thanks to our experienced guide Benjamin Ndjavera, who steered the vehicle confidently, knew the wildlife and plants like his own pocket, nature observations became a unique experience. His humour, charming crew, dignified food and many small details provided varied surprises during the safari. Impressive how nature-loving, luxurious and ecological such a tour can be! Thank you so much!

title: Dreams do come true
text: Best ever Safari experience: Northern Botswana means more wildlife and less people. Mobile tents put you right in the action. Animals galore. Adventure was both rustic and luxe at same time. Excellent, knowledgeable and hard-working guide and camp crew. Spoiled with unbelievable meals and service. Trip of a lifetime.

title: Simply the best. Better than all the rest
text:
Thankyou, thankyou, thankyou.
Our only 'complaint' was that the 9 days were not long enough.
We did the 9 days mobile camping : Chobe, Savuti, Mababe, Moremi.
My sixth Africa trip and by far the best. 
Our guide, Benjamin, was just fabulous. 
Ludig produced delicious, high quality food from the camp kitchen.
Skara attended to our every need, made us laugh, and served a mean G&T.
Polestar made our stay very comfortable with his dedication to all his camp-keeping duties.
We miss everything and everybody : the African skies, the stunning wildlife, the well-selected campsites, the sights/sounds/smells of beautiful Botswana.....and not least the safari team who made it all happen, along with the friends we made as travel companions.

title: The trip of a lifetime!
text: We cannot praise Letaka Safaris enough for our 9 day safari across Moremi, Mababe and Savuti, before ending with a lunchtime cruise on The Chobe River in Kasane. Our guide Shaka’s depth and breadth of experience, coupled with his ability to track animals whilst he was driving, we are sure led to us seeing animals other guides would have missed, and his ability to instantly identify bird species was astounding. The camp crew were warm, friendly and welcoming, and made us feel valued from the start. Keke the camp chef admirably catered for my gluten-free diet, whilst he, Golo and Desa ensured the tented camps were as comfortable as they could be. Golo’s Okavango Gin and tonics were very welcome sundowners, or aperitifs on the evenings when we’d already had sundowners out in the bush. It was just like living in a wildlife documentary on television.

title: Experience of a lifetime!
text:
Our tented safari was a wonderful experience. Even the basic bathroom facilities were part of the adventure. The beds we were provided with were some of the most comfortable we’ve had on any holiday. The camp crew looked after us very well, from housekeeping duties to the food and service around this. Their cheerful greetings were something we looked forward to every time we returned to camp.
Our guide took us to many amazing wildlife sightings and his extensive knowledge/ experience added to this. He was very good at anticipating the wildlife interests of all group members and accommodating these.
We wouldn’t hesitate to travel with Letaka Safaris again.

title: Mobile Safari Moremi to Mababe
text:
We can highly recommend the mobile camping experience across two sites (Moremi Game reserve and Mababe) for 6 days in May.   It was not too hot or too cold, although we had on three layers in the early morning drives.  There were very few mosquitoes present at this time of year.  The equipment, the tents, the bedding, the ensuite and the care of the team was personal, friendly and helpful. The food was fantastic and went beyond expectations. The Landcruiser was so well equipped and comfortable.   

Vincent was an excellent guide very knowledgeable, friendly and professional. The team were friendly, fun and caring. Vincent was attentive and found us animals and birds we were interested in and many more. Each time we went out we saw something different.  We were able to get close to the animals and get great photos without crowds of other vehicles.   The advice on clothing and preparations was accurate and important. Our buffs were, as it turned out, very useful at providing warmth and keeping out sand and wind.

It was a trip of a lifetime and our photos take us back there. Thanks so much Letaka Safaris, Vincent and the team for sharing your beautiful country with us in such a special way.

title: Wonderful experience with Letaka Safaris
text: We just got back from a trip to Botswana and Letaka Safaris was our tour operator for our mobile safari.   Our guide, Benjamin, was so knowledgeable, friendly, kind and funny.  His experience was obvious as he knew the animals’ behavior so well.  The camps were wonderful.  We enjoyed the crew so much.  Ludick, Poster and Skara were a delight.  They were so much fun to be around.  The food was great.  Our vegetarian meals were delicious.  It is truly amazing what they can accomplish in these camps.  There is nothing for you to do but enjoy.  This was a truly awesome experience.  We highly recommend doing a mobile safari with Letaka.

title: Letaka Smooths Out "Roughing It"
text:
We spent a year preparing for our Botswana photo safari. We used "TripAdvisor", "SafariTalk" forum and "Fodors". We chose Botswana because of the country's mega-fauna management style, wealth of mega-fauna (especially elephants) and the country's political stability.

First, we had to choose how to visit. After looking at various possibilities we chose "The Mobile Safari" as our approach. Then we had to choose a Mobile Safari company, there are many.

One name came up in many of the resources we used, Letaka Safari's. We took a deeper look at Letaka, not just their web page either. We bought a copy of the six episode National Geographic presentation "Safari Brothers" that followed the Reed brothers, their father and crew on six different outings (Letaka is a Setswanan word for Reed). Letaka caps their groups at seven guests. We had a total of five on our tour, which ensured great photo opportunities.

We were glad we chose Letaka and for so many reasons. Our guide K G was perfect. He not only got us on animals, he consciously positioned the vehicle so all five of our group had a chance for clear photos. We had a chance to photograph most of the indigenous antelope species from the tiny springbok to the Kudu and Roan. We saw plains zebra, and wildebeests. Elephants like hippos were ubiquitous. 

We had an extended opportunity to photogragh the main pride of the Savuti marsh lions feeding on a kill. We saw a leopard trying unsuccessfully to get into ostrich eggs and another leopard resting with its kill (a springbok) lodged in the fork of a nearby mopane tree. We were lucky enough (thanks to our guide K G) to come upon a mother cheetah with two cubs. Seeing male cheetahs, though rare, is much more likely than seeing a female, especially a female with cubs.

Our rarest animal encounter was the most exciting. We followed an African Wild Dog pack on a hunt and subsequent kill. This lasted for minutes and culminated with them pulling a springhare from a burrow and devouring it before our eyes.

K G then wisely followed the pack, at a distance, until the pack reunited with their pups on an earlier impala kill. We closely observed pack dynamics among the pups as they worked over the impala carcass.

Thanks to K G, we were able to identify over fifty indigenous bird species but there is much more to a Letaka safari than just the animals. There is the luxury of time spent in camp. Every morning started with an early wake up at 5:30 AM. Breakfast was at 6:00 AM and features warm porridge and toast, served with coffee, tea and a variety of fruit juices. This is served on china, silver flatware and napkins. After breakfast, we got in the safari vehicle and searched until around 10:30 AM. Then it's time for morning tea served on the front bumper of the safari vehicle consisting of coffee, tea, various cold beverages and UK style biscuits. 

Around 11:30 AM, we'd return to our camp and welcomed by the camp chef (K B), our waiter Willie, and the camp hand (Karabo sp?) with hoots, hollers, whistles and ululations, not to mention Willie's tray of cool ice teas with a grape and honey resting at the bottom of each glass.

Lunch was  served al fresco under a pitched awning. The table had a table cloth and as I said earlier china plates, flatware, and napkins. One day's lunch menu: homemade bread, pizza, curried cauliflower, fruit salad with raisins and a lettuce salad along with a variety of cold beverages including locally brewed beer. The entire meal was prepared over an open fire. K B the chef never missed providing a spread that would have dazzled anyone in a formal restaurant.

Prior to the the evening meal, we'd have cocktails around the campfire. The evening meal was similar but even more grand than lunch. It started invariably with an appetizer and ended with one of K B's delectable desserts. 

We stayed three nights in each of the three camps we visited. Our tour was the northern highlights and traveled from Chobe National Park in the north, on south to Moremi Game Reserve, and then the northern edge of the Okavanga delta.

Letaka Safaris exceeded our expectations in every possible dimension. Our contact person in setting up the trip, Jonathan, handled every possible question we had and then some. K G's knowledge of the animals and plants seemed limitless and he consistently added context to events unfolding outside our safari vehicle. The camp crew were courteous, welcoming and friendly. The comfort they provided made camp seem like a luxury hotel in the wild. We had an excellent experience with Letaka that we highly recommend.

title: Great camping safari in Botswana
text: An amazing camping safari with Letaka Safaris in and around the Chobe National Park in Botswana. Our guide, Benjamin, displayed great knowledge, enthusiasm and a sense of humour during the nine day trip. Due to his outstanding tracking skills we saw a wide variety of animals and birds. Our party of six guests also enjoyed delicious meals cooked by Vicks, a first rate chef. The other two guys that ran the camp were amazing too. Moving the entire camp three times in nine days to different sites. Sleeping in tents greatly improved the safari experience, and Letaka provided a luxurious service with single or double beds, and an ensuite bathroom with a bucket shower, hand basin and WC. On top of this, we often around sunset had an evening drink in the wild.....such as watching animals gathering around a waterhole.

title: Botswana camping safari
text: Our camping safari with Letaka was brilliant. Our guide, Vincent, was extremely knowledgeable and enthusiastic about all of the animals and birds. It was wonderful to see leopard, lion and wild dog and so many other animals so close. We got some brilliant photographs. The camping gave us the experience of being really close to nature, and was certainly not a hardship, with comfortable beds and delicious food prepared by the camp chef. If you want a safari where great sightings of animals and birds are the priority then Letaka is a fantastic choice.

title: A heart's desire has come true
text: Animals, landscape, sky, Africa and fellow travellers - what a deep and inspiring experience, made possible by an excellent guide Benjamin and his team with Vics, KG and Evidence. We had booked the Northern Unpacked Northbound Safari over 9 days and are still very satisfied with the very special impressions we could make 2 weeks after our return. The competence of the guide, the tents, the jeep, the excellent service and the very good atmosphere in the Letaka team were important positive features of the trip.

title: Letaka Mobile Safari, Okavango, Botswana
text:
Incredible! Our Letaka mobile safari was extraordinary from the start.
First night, just as we had barely sat down to dinner in the bush, a pack of wild dogs raced through camp and killed an impala right in front of us. The wild dogs were followed by a hyena and a jackal. That was a spectacular start to a brilliant trip.
Our guide was excellent, on the morning and afternoon game drives, he tracked lions, leopards, hyenas and more wild dogs with amazing skill, we saw so much wildlife.
The mobile tented camps were in beautiful locations. The camp staff were great and cooked us lovely and varied vegetarian meals and were an awesome team.
Can't recommend the trip highly enough, this was our second safari with Letaka and it was just as good as the first. Thank you Letaka.

title: The best of the best
text: We had an outstanding experience with Letaka. Our safari through Botswana for 9 days was amazing. Our guide, Mbara and camp crew were the best. We never wanted for a single thing and were safe and well cared for. We saw some of the most incredible animals from lions, to wild dogs; hippos and elephants. We would travel with them again. They are the best.

title: Great Letaka Experience
text:
An outstanding trip from beginning to end. A great guide,Benjamin who displayed great knowledge, enthusiasm and sense of humour which helped our group(6) to gel. Hi s driving and tracking were amazing as was the vehicle in which we sought out a huge variety of animals and birds. The three guys who ran the camp were also amazing, moving it three times in nine days to different locations.The bush catering was unbelievable as was their energy end good humour.By the second day they even had g and ts waiting for us when we returned from the evening game drive and hot water was always available for our shower buckets.
 All of the lodges we stayed in were of a very high standard which helped make this an unforgettable experience.

title: Beautiful Botswana
text: We recently returned from a 10 days safari in the Okavango Delta, traveling as a friends group of 6 people. To make a long story short: it was an outstanding experience, best thing ever. Thanks to the wonderful and always cheerful crew: Benjamin, Ludick, Poster and Gula. We always felt safe with Benjamin during the game drives, being an experienced driver and ever so patient to answer a million questions about nature and animals. He always tried to make our day special by finding the best spots for animal sightings. The "magic kitchen crew" with Ludick, Poster and Gula surprised us with fresh bread, cookies and cakes from the bush oven! Moreover, who would expect hot water bottles wrapped in blankets at 6:30 am in the open vehicle? Boys, you made us feel very comfy. Thank you all for a wonderful Botswana experience!

title: A great Safari in Botswana.
text:
Our first visit to Africa. Our second day in Africa we began our Letaka Safari. From the beginning to the end it was amazing. Our guide, Benjamin, answered all the questions, provided excellent and clear information, tracked all the animals we wanted to see and was fun. The camp crew, KG, Vicks and Evidence were lovely, efficient, attentive, caring and also fun. 
The camp was comfortable, the food copious and delicious. What more can a Safari be. We had a fantastic experience on our 10 Northern Highlights tour.

title: Letaka Safaris is the BEST
text: I can't say enough about Letaka Safaris.  I've been with them 4 times now, over 10 years, and each time was an incredible and unique experience.  After doing safaris in Kenya, Tanzania, South Africa and Botswana I can say Botswana is head and shoulders above the rest and Letaka is the best company I've experienced.  Letaka is a bit rough, but you hardly notice that because you're so well cared for.  The food is always great and the tents are very comfortable.  The most amazing thing is the level of expertise of the guides, who are incredibly knowledgeable about every bird or animal you will come across.  They can track anything, as they drive along, and can identify any bird from it's call.  I plan to continue with safaris every couple of years and would not go with any other company.
